abstract: Releases the memory used by the keychain attribute list and the keychain data retrieved in a call to the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainItemCopyContent(_:_:_:_:_:)] function.

declaration.code: func SecKeychainItemFreeContent(_ attrList: UnsafeMutablePointer<SecKeychainAttributeList>?, _ data: UnsafeMutableRawPointer?) -> OSStatus

overview:
## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

Because the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainFindInternetPassword(_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:)] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainFindGenericPassword(_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:)] functions call the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainItemCopyContent(_:_:_:_:_:)] function, you must call `SecKeychainItemFreeContent` to release the data buffers after calls to those functions as well.

Because the `SecKeychainItemCopyContent` function does not allocate buffers until they are needed, you should not call the `SecKeychainItemFreeContent` function unless data is actually returned to you.

# SecKeychainItemFreeContent(_:_:)

**Function**

Releases the memory used by the keychain attribute list and the keychain data retrieved in a call to the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainItemCopyContent(_:_:_:_:_:)] function.

## Declaration

```swift
func SecKeychainItemFreeContent(_ attrList: UnsafeMutablePointer<SecKeychainAttributeList>?, _ data: UnsafeMutableRawPointer?) -> OSStatus
```

## Parameters

- **attrList**: A pointer to the attribute list to release. Pass `NULL` if there is no attribute list to release.
- **data**: A pointer to the data buffer to release. Pass `NULL` if there is no data to release.

## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

Because the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainFindInternetPassword(_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:)] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainFindGenericPassword(_:_:_:_:_:_:_:_:)] functions call the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainItemCopyContent(_:_:_:_:_:)] function, you must call `SecKeychainItemFreeContent` to release the data buffers after calls to those functions as well.

Because the `SecKeychainItemCopyContent` function does not allocate buffers until they are needed, you should not call the `SecKeychainItemFreeContent` function unless data is actually returned to you.
